title: Reproducible Repository, Toolchain, and Dependency Stewardship · Product Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for reproducible repository, toolchain, and dependency stewardship and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reproducible healthy project repository. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.
Pin supported runtime, package manager, compiler, formatter, linter, type checker, test runner, and build behavior with compatibility evidence.
Evaluate dependency need, maintenance, provenance, license, advisories, transitive cost, API stability, and replacement or removal path.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Repository Contract
steps.instruction:
Order the Repository Contract evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Repository Contract.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Repository Contract assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Repository Contract, keep Repository Contract connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A promising product works only in a warm local directory with floating dependencies, an exposed sample token, unclear license, and no recovery anchor. The quiz variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Repository Contract has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Repository Contract, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Repository Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Repository Contract, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A promising product works only in a warm local directory with floating dependencies, an exposed sample token, unclear license, and no recovery anchor. The quiz variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Repository Contract, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A README installation snippet is a complete repository contract.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the quiz Repository Contract case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the quiz Repository Contract case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the quiz Repository Contract case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the quiz Repository Contract case

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Clean Reproduction
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Clean Reproduction: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A promising product works only in a warm local directory with floating dependencies, an exposed sample token, unclear license, and no recovery anchor. The quiz variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Prove clone-to-gates-to-build behavior in a clean environment and record exact revision, lock, tool, artifact, and failure evidence.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Clean Reproduction.
steps.why: Retrieving Clean Reproduction through the concrete reproducible healthy project repository case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Clean Reproduction, keep Clean Reproduction connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove clone-to-gates-to-build behavior in a clean environment and record exact revision, lock, tool, artifact, and failure evidence.
